name: opencode-extreme-compress
install_doc: git clone https://github.com/mifdlaldev/opencode-extreme-compress.git && cd opencode-extreme-compress && bun install && bun run build && ./scripts/install.sh
notes: OpenCode plugin with 3-layer compression: L1=tool-output truncation (head/tail, 200+50 lines, 100KB cap); L2=regex comment stripping from .ts/.js/.py files (not tree-sitter); L3=LLM-based semantic summarizer triggered after N turns (medium+ modes only, requires separate LLM call). Mode ladder: off/shadow/light/medium/extreme. Light is default (L1+L2 only, no LLM call). JSONC config at ~/.config/opencode/extreme-compress.jsonc with per-model mode profiles. Includes standalone TUI (packages/tui) for real-time stats monitoring (reads ~/.config/opencode/extreme-compress-stats.jsonl). Anti-hallucination charter: 6 principles + 15 blocking tests (never lose paths/identifiers/numbers, fail-safe=no-compression, marker on truncation). Cache-safe: never rewrites conversation history; only mutates tool output in-place via tool.execute.after and experimental.chat.messages.transform hooks. Target: OpenCode only. Claimed 40-92% reduction, but self-admits 'measured in unit tests — real-session benchmarks pending'. L4 reference-based dedup (SQLite) not yet implemented.
